subject: [PATCH v2] mm/huge_memory: set PG_has_hwpoisoned only after new folio head is established

reviews.author: Lorenzo Stoakes
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch's logic looks good after the fix and appreciated the improved comment, thus giving a Reviewed-by tag.
reviews.raw_body:
The actual logic looks good, thanks for fixing this, and the comment is much
nicer now :) So:

Reviewed-by: Lorenzo Stoakes <ljs@kernel.org>

2026-07-02.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the Linux kernel's huge memory handling by moving the setting of PG_has_hwpoisoned to after the new folio head is established, ensuring that the flag is set on a valid folio head. This prevents a kernel BUG from occurring when trying to split a poisoned folio to order 0. The patch addresses an issue where the hwpoison state was being copied onto sub-folios before they were properly set up as heads, leading to incorrect behavior and crashes.

2026-07-03.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the Linux kernel's huge memory handling by moving the setting of PG_has_hwpoisoned to after the new folio head is established, ensuring that the flag is set on a valid folio head. This prevents a kernel BUG from occurring when trying to split a poisoned folio to order 0. The patch addresses an issue where the hwpoison state was being copied onto sub-folios before they were properly set up as heads, leading to incorrect behavior and crashes.
